title: The Trump Supporters Who Say He Went Too Far on Immigration
title_cn: 那些认为特朗普在移民问题上走得太远的特朗普支持者

paragraphs.cn: 美国东部时间2026年1月30日晚9:00

paragraphs.en: PHOENIX—Maricela Garcia has always considered herself a conservative, so supporting President Trump in 2024 was the obvious choice, driven in part by his promise to deport criminals who are in the country illegally. The 37-year-old insurance agent said she never expected that just a year later his immigration policies would touch her family.
paragraphs.cn: 凤凰城——马里塞拉·加西亚一直认为自己是保守派，因此在2024年支持特朗普总统是显而易见的选择，部分原因是特朗普承诺驱逐非法滞留在美国的罪犯。这位37岁的保险经纪人表示，她从未想到仅仅一年后，他的移民政策会影响到她的家人。

paragraphs.en: Last summer, Garcia’s husband, an immigrant from the Dominican Republic, was detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement for three months, she said. Her husband, who came to the U.S. on a tourist visa and was in the process of applying for legal status, is now barred from working since his work permit was confiscated even though it remains valid, she said. He must wear an ankle monitor and rarely leaves their house for fear of another run-in with agents.
paragraphs.cn: 她说，去年夏天，加西亚的丈夫，一名来自多米尼加共和国的移民，被移民及海关执法局拘留了三个月。她说，她的丈夫持旅游签证来到美国，正在申请合法身份，但由于他的工作许可证被没收，尽管仍然有效，他现在被禁止工作。他必须佩戴脚踝监视器，并且很少离开家，生怕再次与特工发生冲突。

paragraphs.en: Trump “is literally tearing America apart,” Garcia said, adding that she regrets her vote. “He is dividing us.”
paragraphs.cn: 加西亚说，特朗普“简直是在撕裂美国”，并补充说她后悔投了他的票。“他正在分裂我们。”

paragraphs.en: Garcia is one of a growing number of Americans, including those who voted for the president, who are souring on his immigration agenda amid tactics that have led to clashes with protesters in places including Minneapolis. The shifting view on Trump’s handling of immigration comes after he tapped into voters’ concerns around the issue to win back the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 加西亚是越来越多的美国人之一，其中包括那些投票给总统的人，他们对特朗普的移民议程感到不满，因为其策略导致在包括明尼阿波利斯在内的多个地方与抗议者发生冲突。对特朗普处理移民问题的看法转变，发生在他利用选民对该问题的担忧重新赢得白宫之后。

paragraphs.en:

vocabulary.meaning_en: Holding to traditional attitudes and values and cautious about change or innovation, typically in relation to politics or religion.
vocabulary.example: Maricela Garcia has always considered herself a conservative, so supporting President Trump in 2024 was the obvious choice, driven in part by his promise to deport criminals who are in the country illegally.

vocabulary.meaning_en: Expel (a foreigner) from a country, typically on the grounds of illegal status or for having committed a crime.
vocabulary.example: Maricela Garcia has always considered herself a conservative, so supporting President Trump in 2024 was the obvious choice, driven in part by his promise to deport criminals who are in the country illegally.

vocabulary.meaning_en: Keep (someone) in official custody, typically for questioning about a crime or before a trial.
vocabulary.example: Last summer, Garcia’s husband, an immigrant from the Dominican Republic, was detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement for three months, she said.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她表示，去年夏天，加西亚的丈夫，一名来自多米尼加共和国的移民，被移民及海关执法局拘留了三个月。

vocabulary.example: Last summer, Garcia’s husband, an immigrant from the Dominican Republic, was detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement for three months, she said.

vocabulary.example: Her husband, who came to the U.S. on a tourist visa and was in the process of applying for legal status, is now barred from working since his work permit was confiscated even though it remains valid, she said.

vocabulary.meaning_en: Feel sad, repentant, or disappointed over (something that has happened or been done, especially a loss or missed opportunity).
vocabulary.example: Trump “is literally tearing America apart,” Garcia said, adding that she regrets her vote.
